identity._doc: Unique identity of the agent. Required fields: name, version, type.
identity.name: my-agent
identity.description: Brief description of the agent and its purpose.
identity.author: Your Name or Org

runtime._doc: Technical runtime configuration. Local models preferred (local-first).

skills._doc: Declared capabilities of the agent. Used for self-description and routing in mesh.
skills._note: shell_execution is NOT listed here because permissions.process.shell_execution is false. A capability without the matching permission is effectively disabled (see SPEC: Permissions vs. Capabilities).

custom_skills.name: slack_notify
custom_skills.description: Sends status messages to a Slack channel (e.g. after deployments or session closings).

custom_skills.name: bootstrap_workspace
custom_skills.description: Idempotent WORKING/ structure creation. Reads .agent.json, checks folder existence, creates missing folders and seed files (READ-AGENT.md, ltm-index.md). Never deletes. Safe to run multiple times.

repo_root.description: Absolute path to the repository root.

dry_run.description: If true, report what would be created without making changes. Default: false.

skipped.description: Already existing paths that were not touched.

permissions._doc: Explicit permissions and prohibitions. Everything not explicitly permitted is forbidden.

filesystem._restricted_write_doc: Files the agent may only modify with explicit user confirmation. .gitignore is here because modifying it could bypass security patterns.

memory._doc: Persistence and storage strategy of the agent. Paths are derived from workspace.structure (Single Source of Truth).

long_term._note: Vector store is the queryable LTM from session 1. Human-readable audit log is at workspace.structure.memory (ltm-index.md). Both are maintained in parallel.

session._doc: Session logging and audit protocol. workpaper_path uses workspace.structure.workpapers as base.
session._workpaper_path_doc: Fallback path when no topic is known (e.g. first session, onboarding). For topic-specific workpapers, use workspace.workpaper_rules.naming_pattern instead ({date}-{agent}-{topic}.md).

tools._doc: External tools and API bindings the agent is allowed to use.

registry.name: project_search
registry.description: Semantic search over project documentation. API key is read from environment variable SEARCH_API_KEY.

registry.name: local_linter
registry.description: Local linter for code quality checks.

workspace._doc: Complete work instructions. The agent clones a repo, finds AGENT.json, and sets itself up according to these rules.
workspace._auto_create_doc: Set to false for read-only setups where the agent should not create or modify folders.

structure._doc: Folders the agent creates automatically. Each path relative to project root.

onboarding._doc: Steps the agent executes during initial setup — in this order.

steps.description: Read PROJECT-ANALYSIS.md if present — contains pre-manifest project reality assessment (repo topology, existing tools, LTM state, workpaper history, security enforcement, governance). Informs all subsequent onboarding decisions.
Read READ-AGENT.md — provides project context, architecture, priorities.
Create all folders from workspace.structure if they don't exist.
Scan entire repository — capture files, languages, dependencies, existing documentation.

sections: File inventory (directory tree, file count by type)
Build/run commands (how to start the project)

steps.description: Create READ-AGENT.md if not present — project structure, core architecture, start commands, reading list.
Derive coding standards and architecture rules from the project and document in GUIDELINES/.
Index all documentation (whitepapers, READMEs, code comments) into long-term memory.
Create first workpaper: onboarding protocol with repository scan results.

workpaper_rules._doc: Rules for how workpapers are written and closed. Based on real-world experience from 60+ sessions.

file_tracking._doc: File protocol — the heart of session hygiene. Without it, nobody knows what happened.

closing_checklist: No temporary test files in repo (test-*, debug-*, temp-*)?
No commented-out code blocks without explanation?
No secrets/passwords/tokens in plain text in workpaper?
Architecture decisions noted in corresponding whitepaper?
Discarded work branches explicitly marked as ABANDONED?
Cleanup tasks for next session named?

code_hygiene._doc: Rules for clean code. Problem from practice: agents solve tasks well but don't clean up.

secrets_policy._doc: Workpapers end up as context in AI prompts. Everything in them is potentially accessible in plain text.

ltm_triggers.description: LTM audit log has 90+ entries and no vector store found. Agent notifies developer: without WORKING/AGENT-MEMORY/ older context becomes blind above 100 entries. Recommended action: pip install chromadb — agent will ingest automatically on next session start.

LTM audit log exceeds 100 entries with no vector store present. Agent loads only last 20 entries and states this explicitly at session start. This is the fallback — the intended path is WORKING/AGENT-MEMORY/ from session 1.
Before starting a new work session: load context from LTM.
On every new chat: search session topic in LTM.
Chat context limit reached: ingest, then query in new chat.
Workpaper closed: ingest BEFORE moving to close/.
Whitepapers or workpapers changed: re-ingest at session end.
On code changes: check standards and architecture patterns in LTM.
New package installed: ingest if documentation-relevant.
Before saving any workpaper: scan content for secret patterns (API keys, tokens, passwords, private keys). Block if match found. This is the last guardrail before a secret enters Git history.

workspace._gitignore_note: WORKING/MEMORY/ is intentionally NOT ignored — ltm-index.md is the shared collaboration artifact. Only runtime/binary artifacts are excluded. When using a vector store, add: WORKING/AGENT-MEMORY/, WORKING/MEMORY/*.db, WORKING/MEMORY/chroma/

file_safety._doc: Optional file safety guidance. Describes how agents may handle file deletions outside the workspace.

outside_workspace._doc: Files outside WORKING/ should be treated with caution before deletion.
outside_workspace.if_history_found: Files with git history may be relevant. Inform the user before deletion.
outside_workspace.if_no_history: Files without git history may be safe to delete.
outside_workspace.manual_rm_rf: Never use rm -rf with manual file lists without git verification.

skills._doc: Optional. Skills are crystallized community knowledge. Agents may use them as starting points, not as requirements. AAMS is neutral and standardless: describes WHAT a skill CAN be, not HOW to implement it.

global_pool._doc: A global pool of skills is the source of truth. Not to be modified — only referenced.

local_adaptation._doc: When a skill is adapted for a project, it carries its origin as a header comment (# source: origin/skill-name) and may be tailored to the project context.

issue_suggestion._doc: When a detected tool has no skills in the builder, AAMS may suggest creating a new skill entry.

relationship._doc: Global → Local: Source → Refinement. Exploration → direct reach.

tool_detection._doc: AAMS detects the calling agent/tool at runtime. This enables tool-specific skill recommendations. Detection method depends on the tool. AAMS does NOT prescribe how detection happens — only: if you CAN detect, use it.

examples.lm-studio: LM Studio running on localhost (default port 1234), OpenAI-compatible endpoint
examples.ollama: ollama serve running on localhost (default port 11434)
examples.llama.cpp: llama-server or llama.cpp binary running, GGUF model loaded

tool_detection._doc_local_llm: AAMS voll unterstützt lokale LLMs. Unser Setup: LM Studio + qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b. Manchmal Ollama, manchmal llama.cpp direkt. Du entscheidest welches Tool und welches Modell. AAMS ist neutral.

security._doc: Optional security metadata. Describes the security posture of the agent and its environment. Trust-portability: security history travels with the agent.

guard._doc: Optional guard configuration for contract enforcement. Describes WHAT to check, not HOW. Each tool implements differently.

manifest_read._doc: Before any write/edit operation, verify the agent has read the manifest contract.
manifest_read.description: Agents should confirm they've loaded the manifest before making changes.

workpaper_open._doc: Before any write/edit operation, verify an active workpaper exists.
workpaper_open.check: WORKING/WORKPAPER/ contains a file with status: open (or tool-equivalent)
workpaper_open.description: All changes should be documented in a session-bound workpaper.

tools_gated._doc: write/edit/apply_patch/bash should be blocked until manifest_read AND workpaper_open are satisfied.

error_format._doc: Error messages describe the state, not the command. Descriptive, not imperative.
error_format.manifest_not_read: GUARD-VERSTOSS: Lies zuerst .agent.json und READ-AGENT.md, bevor du Änderungen vornimmst.
error_format.no_workpaper: GUARD-VERSTOSS: Kein offenes Workpaper unter WORKING/. Lege zuerst ein Workpaper mit status: open an.

implementation._doc: Tool-specific implementations are local adaptations. See WORKING/TOOLS/ for reference implementations.

metadata._doc: Free-form field for provider-specific extensions and project-specific data. No fixed schema.

_deviations.reason: Pre-AAMS naming convention — 50+ historical workpapers use 'close/', renaming would break cross-references.
Historical consistency — naming pattern predates AAMS adoption.
